title: Harden task clean against pnpm symlink deletion flakes on macOS
description:
`task clean` (Taskfile.yml clean target) runs `rm -rf web/node_modules web/build web/.svelte-kit` which intermittently fails on macOS with 'Directory not empty' against pnpm's deeply nested .pnpm/ symlink store. Repro on PR #925 validation: ran fine the first time, then failed on subsequent task pr-prep invocations after web:build re-installed node_modules, breaking the chain.

## Fix options

A) Replace `rm -rf web/node_modules` with `find web/node_modules -depth -delete` (works on pnpm's symlink chains).
B) Wrap the rm with a retry loop (pragmatic but ugly).
C) Scope task clean to NOT touch node_modules — most projects don't include node_modules in clean, since pnpm's content-addressable store amortizes reinstalls.

Prefer option (C) — least magic, most predictable. Add task clean:deep that does include node_modules for full reset cases.

## Acceptance criteria

- task pr-prep can run twice in a row without manual intervention.
- task clean is idempotent on macOS regardless of whether web/node_modules exists or has been partially populated.
- Documented in Taskfile.yml comments why node_modules is excluded from default clean.

title: runServe probe-wiring integration test + startProbeListener extraction
description:
Surfaced during PR #916 review (pr-test-analyzer P7). The probe-listener wiring in cmd/specgraph/serve.go (bind via net.Listen, Serve in goroutine, parallel shutdown) has no automated coverage. Every refactor risks breaking one of: default-off behavior, bind-failure-aborts-startup, ctx-cancel-triggers-shutdown, actual livez/readyz routing.

Step 1: extract a helper 'startProbeListener(ctx, pinger, addr) (*http.Server, error)' that returns the started server or a bind error. Keeps runServe readable and makes the probe logic unit-testable.

Step 2: add tests in cmd/specgraph/ (or e2e/api/probes_test.go if we want full runServe spawn):
  - TestRunServe_ProbesDisabledByDefault — Listen="" → no port opened.
  - TestRunServe_ProbesServeLivezAndReadyz — Listen set, GET /livez → 200, GET /readyz eventually → 200 w/ healthy pinger.
  - TestRunServe_BindFailureAbortsStartup — Listen set to already-bound port → runServe returns error (don't silently continue with a broken probe).
  - TestRunServe_ProbesShutDownOnCtxCancel — after ctx cancel, port closes within shutdown timeout.

title: Postgres Store.Ping integration test (testcontainer)
description:
Surfaced during PR #916 review (pr-test-analyzer P7). internal/storage/postgres/postgres.go gained Store.Ping(ctx) as a 3-line pgxpool.Ping pass-through — the only wire between 'probe says ready' and 'Postgres is actually reachable.' Zero test coverage today. A refactor could silently make /readyz always return 200.

Tests to add in internal/storage/postgres/ (//go:build integration tag, uses existing testcontainer harness):
  - TestStore_Ping_ReturnsNilWhenPoolHealthy — standard bootstrap, assert nil.
  - TestStore_Ping_ReturnsErrorWhenPoolClosed — after store.Close(), assert non-nil. Proves method exercises the pool, not a no-op stub.
  - TestStore_Ping_RespectsContextCancel — pass cancelled ctx, assert context.Canceled (or wraps it). Catches regressions to non-ctx-respecting Ping variants.

title: Probe readiness: log ping transitions (ready↔not-ready) instead of discarding errors
description:
Surfaced during PR #916 review (silent-failure-hunter HIGH, code-reviewer S5, type-design-analyzer). internal/server/probes/probes.go:51-55 currently does 'h.ready.Store(pinger.Ping(pingCtx) == nil)' — every error mode (pool exhaustion, DNS failure, auth error, deadline exceeded) collapses to a single boolean. Operators seeing /readyz=503 have zero in-process signal about why.

Desired: log on state transitions only. Two slog calls per outage (ready→not-ready with error, not-ready→ready), not a flood. Probe() becomes:

  err := pinger.Ping(pingCtx)
  prev := h.ready.Swap(err == nil)
  switch {
  case err != nil && prev:
    slog.Warn("readiness probe failed", "error", err)
  case err == nil && !prev:
    slog.Info("readiness probe recovered")
  }

Optional extension: store last error on an atomic.Pointer[error] field and return it in /readyz body so 'curl /readyz' gives operators a reason string. Requires changing the Pinger wrapping in storage/postgres to include fmt.Errorf("postgres: ping: %w", ...) (remove the //nolint:wrapcheck bypass).

title: Test: assert non-loopback-without-auth warning fires for default 0.0.0.0:9090
description:
Surfaced during PR #915 review (pr-test-analyzer P6 suggestion). cmd/specgraph/serve.go:202 logs slog.Warn("server listening without authentication on non-loopback interface") when !HasAuth && !isLoopbackAddr(cfg.Server.Listen). After #915 this warning is the default out-of-box experience for every unconfigured operator (0.0.0.0:9090 binds on all interfaces). The warning path has no test coverage — nothing captures slog output to verify it fires.

Desired: add a slogtest-based unit test in cmd/specgraph/serve_test.go that captures the handler output and runs the pre-flight auth/listen block (or a factored-out helper like shouldWarnNoAuth(cfg) bool). Asserts the warning fires for the new default. Catches regressions where a refactor drops the warning silently.

Shape: likely requires a minor extraction — runServe is currently a 250-line function that isn't unit-testable for the warning branch. Either (a) extract a small helper, or (b) use slog.SetDefault + a testing handler for the whole runServe call with an early-exit stub for Postgres connect.

Related: #913 (the default change that elevates this gap).

title: LoadGlobal should not auto-create defaults when --config is explicitly set
description:
Surfaced during PR #914 review. internal/config/global.go:90-102 writes defaults to path when the file is missing. Pre-#914 this only fired for XDG default and legacy .specgraph/config.yaml (both user-writable, both expected). Post-#914, --config also reaches LoadGlobal, so an operator running 'specgraph serve --config /etc/specgraph/config.yaml' with a missing/mistyped path gets one of: (a) silent default-config materialization at the operator-supplied path (worst case), or (b) a 'write default config: <perm>' error on read-only mounts (already covered by TestLoadGlobal_ReadOnlyParentDir).

Desired: when path is user-supplied (via --config), LoadGlobal should error out with a clear 'config file not found at <path>' message. When path is the XDG default (no flag), retain current auto-create behavior.

Shape: likely takes a second arg or a second constructor (e.g. LoadGlobalExplicit(path)) so the caller signals 'this is a user-specified path, don't synthesize'. Then globalConfigPath() in cmd/specgraph/main.go returns (path, explicit bool) and serve/up/down/prime route accordingly.

title: Per-workspace XDG isolation to prevent shared-volume footgun across jj workspaces
description:
Today $XDG_DATA_HOME/specgraph/ is per-user, per-machine. Two jj workspaces of this repo — prescribed by CLAUDE.md for parallel work — share a single compose file and Postgres volume. Consequences:

- 'specgraph up' in workspaces A and B connect to the same database.
- 'specgraph down --purge' in A destroys B's data.
- 'specgraph uninstall' in A stops the service B relied on.

Phase 3 follow-up from 2026-04-22-cli-lifecycle-split-design.md. The Phase 2 confirmation prompt mentions the shared state but doesn't prevent it. Needs a per-workspace XDG directory scheme (probably under $XDG_DATA_HOME/specgraph/<repo-hash>/).

Related files:
- cmd/specgraph/ (all lifecycle commands read/write XDG paths)
- internal/xdg/ (core path derivation)
- internal/docker/compose.go (compose file location)

Not a blocker for Phase 1/2 of the lifecycle split; the destructive path is now user-guarded.

title: Contribute SessionWithTools to mcp-go stdioSession
description:
mcp-go v0.45.0's stdioSession does not implement server.SessionWithTools. This means the OnAfterInitialize hook cannot call SetSessionTools for stdio sessions, forcing a workaround in internal/mcp/server.go where WithProfileOverride registers only filtered tools at construction time instead of filtering uniformly via the hook.

The correct design is transport-agnostic: always register all tools on the MCPServer, always filter via OnAfterInitialize. stdioSession already implements SessionWithClientInfo, SessionWithLogging, SessionWithSampling, SessionWithElicitation, and SessionWithRoots — adding SessionWithTools is a natural, small addition.

Action: contribute SessionWithTools support to stdioSession in github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go, then remove the conditional tool registration workaround in server.go.
close_reason: Won't fix — stdio transport dropped from MCP server (spgr-o3jn). HTTP-only architecture removes need for upstream SessionWithTools contribution.

title: Add load-time validation for publish config fields
description: CodeRabbit review on PR #896 suggested validating Auth.Method (api_token|oauth2) and PollInterval (parseable duration, default 15m) during config Load(). Currently these are only schema-documented, not validated at load time. Runtime validation exists in serve.go for required fields (CloudID, SpaceKey, APIToken, UserEmail).

title: Add optimistic concurrency version guard to UpdateDecision
description: UpdateDecision currently matches only on slug without checking version. Add expected_version to UpdateDecisionRequest proto and a WHERE d.version = $expected_version guard in the Cypher query, returning ErrConcurrentModification on mismatch. Same pattern as Spec updates (ADR-004). From CodeRabbit review on PR #812.

Convert with 'go tool covdata textfmt' and upload to Codecov. This captures the actual code paths exercised by e2e CLI tests through the real binary.

Needs a batch GetSpecs query or pagination strategy to cap query volume. Affects internal/drift/drift.go checkSpec loop and potentially storage.SpecReader interface.

description: SpecStage conflates authoring funnel stages (spark, shape, specify, decompose, approved, in_progress, review, done, amended) with terminal lifecycle states (superseded, abandoned). This makes it impossible to distinguish at the type level between stages that represent forward progress and states that represent finality. Splitting into separate types would improve type safety and prevent invalid transitions at compile time. Ripple: proto definitions, storage interfaces, handlers, converters, CLI, and all tests.

title: Promote probe interval/timeout from consts to ProbesConfig
description:
Surfaced during PR #916 review (type-design-analyzer + pr-test-analyzer). cmd/specgraph/serve.go has probeInterval=5s and probeTimeout=2s as package-level consts. Docs at site/docs/guides/health-probes.md invite operators to 'file an issue if your workload requires different timing' — this is that follow-up.

Shape: add fields to internal/config.ProbesConfig:
  type ProbesConfig struct {
    Listen       string        
    Interval     time.Duration 
    ProbeTimeout time.Duration 
  }

Load path resolves zero values to the current defaults (5s interval, 2s timeout). Also add Validate() — both must be positive; probeTimeout should be ≤ interval (not strictly required but defensive). Update the Tuning Notes section of health-probes.md to document the new YAML keys.

Covers: (1) Pagination/batching for drift check-all O(N×D) queries (was spgr-bnu), (2) Rate limiting for bulk Lint and CheckDrift (was spgr-lud), (3) Batch/streaming GetSpecs for bulk drift ack merge (was spgr-87m), (4) Design sharding/backup/archive/pruning strategy (was spgr-0i6). None are needed until graph size grows significantly.

title: Evaluate alternative graph backends: Neo4j, Postgres+RDF, Nebula, JanusGraph
description: Investigate whether Neo4j or another graph database should replace or supplement Memgraph. Consolidates spgr-8nf and spgr-dag. Evaluate: Neo4j (most mature, enterprise features), Postgres+AGE/RDF (operational simplicity), Nebula (distributed), JanusGraph (pluggable storage). Consider: query compatibility, Bolt protocol support, licensing, operational overhead, migration path from Memgraph.
notes: Decision: pure Postgres/SQL. Analysis showed ~80% of operations are trivially relational, ~15% need recursive CTEs (transitive deps, impact, critical path), ~5% need careful SQL. Operational simplicity wins over graph query expressiveness. Clean seam at internal/storage/ interfaces makes this a contained rewrite.

title: Web UI: syntax highlighting and code block rendering for spec content

Content often contains code snippets, API signatures, and structured data that would benefit from code block formatting and optionally syntax highlighting. Evaluate: (1) markdown rendering for spec text fields (intent, notes, exchanges), (2) code block detection and monospace rendering for interface bodies and pre blocks, (3) lightweight syntax highlighting (e.g., highlight.js or Shiki) for known languages. Keep bundle size reasonable — this is an embedded SPA served via go:embed.

title: Gastown spec version tracking integration

title: ChangeLog compaction for long-lived specs

title: Design vector store integration for semantic search
description: Add an optional vector store capability to SpecGraph for semantic similarity search across specs. This complements the existing Memgraph graph traversal with fuzzy cross-referencing, duplicate detection, and conceptual drift analysis. Design work includes: (1) Define a SemanticSearcher storage interface, (2) Evaluate embeddable vector stores (SQLite-vec, Qdrant, pgvector), (3) Design embedding model abstraction (accept pre-computed embeddings, support local and API-based models), (4) Plan integration with drift/driftscope, (5) Write ADR documenting the decision. Should be optional, not a hard dependency — keep SpecGraph local-first.

title: Design: One-way export to Confluence for specs, decisions, and project artifacts
description: Design a one-way export pipeline from SpecGraph to Confluence. Publish a project's specs, decisions (ADRs), constitution, and graph relationships as Confluence pages/spaces. Consider: page hierarchy mapping (space → project, pages → specs/decisions), Confluence storage format (XHTML), macro usage for metadata/status, incremental vs full export, page update strategy (overwrite vs versioned), cross-linking between exported pages, table of contents / index pages, and authentication (API tokens, OAuth).

title: Design: Chat integration (Slack/Discord) for authoring + issue tracker handoff
description:
Brainstorm and design a Slack-native interface to SpecGraph, covering:

**Slack Bot Core:**
- Bot that exposes the full authoring funnel (spark → shape → specify → decompose → approve) via conversational Slack interactions
- Query system accessible through slash commands or bot mentions (e.g., `/specgraph blocked`, `@specgraph show oauth-refresh`)
- Threaded conversations for spec authoring — each thread maps to a spec's funnel progression
- Notifications on stage transitions, review requests, approval flows

**Inter-Bot Integration:**
- How the SpecGraph Slack bot could integrate with Claude (Anthropic's Slack bot) for AI-assisted authoring — e.g., user starts a spec thread, Claude bot helps shape/specify, SpecGraph bot captures the structured output
- Integration with Cursor's Slack presence or other coding assistant bots
- Bot-to-bot handoff patterns: user invokes SpecGraph bot, it delegates to Claude for creative work, captures results back into the spec graph
- MCP server exposure through Slack (could the bot act as a bridge to SpecGraph's MCP/ConnectRPC API?)

**Query & Visibility:**
- Slack-native spec browsing (interactive messages, Block Kit for spec cards)
- Status dashboards posted to channels (e.g., daily digest of blocked specs, funnel progress)
- Deep linking from Slack messages back to SpecGraph CLI/web views

**Architecture Considerations:**
- Slack Events API vs. Socket Mode
- Bot vs. webhook architecture for different interaction patterns
- How to handle long-running authoring sessions across multiple Slack messages
- Authentication: mapping Slack users to SpecGraph identities
- Rate limiting and Slack API constraints for spec-heavy teams

Previously also scoped issue tracker handoff (GitHub Issues, Linear, Jira, beads) — that remains in scope as the "push/pull" mechanism for approved specs.

title: Design: Import specs from external spec systems (SpecKit, BMAD, OpenSpec, Superpowers)
description: Brainstorm and design the import pipeline for bringing specs from other spec systems into SpecGraph. Systems to consider: SpecKit, BMAD, OpenSpec, obra Superpowers. Need to define: source formats, mapping to SpecGraph domain model, edge preservation, authoring funnel entry point, conflict resolution, and CLI UX.

title: Test preconditionError catch-all path
description: Add integration test exercising preconditionError catch-all branch (unexplained guard failure). Requires Memgraph testcontainer. Deferred from PR #27 review finding spgr-4kq.18.
close_reason: Addressed in turn 4 review (spgr-0fk.3). Catch-all is defense-in-depth only reachable via Cypher bugs, tested at handler level.

title: Add ADR or plan doc for early deployment profile work
description:
The Memgraph sizing profiles (deploy/memgraph/) shipped in Phase 1/2 while the CLAUDE.md roadmap places 'Scale' work in Phase 4. This is intentional (low-risk, additive, user-requested) but should be documented with a brief ADR or plan entry explaining the rationale for pulling this work forward.

From PR #23 review finding spgr-bke.8.

title: Add Memgraph authentication and TLS to deployment profiles
description:
All deployment profiles (local-dev through large-org) and the compose.go init template currently run Memgraph with no authentication and no TLS on the bolt endpoint. Security warning comments were added in PR #23, but actual enforcement is deferred.

Work needed:
1. Configure Memgraph authentication (--auth-module-mappings, CREATE USER) in medium-org and large-org profiles
2. Add TLS configuration for bolt endpoint (--bolt-cert-file, --bolt-key-file) in medium-org and large-org profiles
3. Add MEMGRAPH_USER / MEMGRAPH_PASSWORD env vars to compose files
4. Update the Go client connection code in internal/storage/memgraph/ to pass credentials and TLS config from StorageConfig
5. Update documentation (README, compose.go comments) with auth setup instructions
6. local-dev and small-org can remain NoAuth with localhost binding as a reasonable default

Pre-1.0 requirement. Not needed during Phase 1-3 development.

title: Design sharding, backup, archive, and pruning strategy for Memgraph at scale
description:
Memgraph is memory-only, so large orgs (1K+ engineers, 500+ apps) need formal strategies for:

1. **Sharding** — define how to partition the graph across multiple Memgraph instances (by domain, by application portfolio, or by tenant). Document the routing layer, cross-shard edge handling, and federation queries.

2. **Backup** — formalize snapshot export strategy beyond WAL/snapshots. Define RPO/RTO targets per tier. Consider Memgraph's built-in snapshot mechanism vs external dump tooling.

3. **Archive** — define lifecycle for superseded/done specs. When should they be exported from the live graph? What format (JSONL, Cypher dump)? Where do they go (object storage, secondary read-only instance)?

4. **Pruning** — define automated rules for removing stale data (expired claims, orphaned Agent nodes, old analytical pass JSON blobs on completed specs).

This is Phase 4 (Scale) work per the roadmap but the design should be documented earlier so the data model doesn't paint us into a corner.
notes: Timeline: not blocking Phase 4, but must be completed before 1.0 release.

content-hash-shipped: PR #39 merged. Spec and Decision nodes now have a content_hash field (Murmur3-128, 32 hex chars) computed from substantive fields. recomputeContentHash returns (string, error) and is called from all stage-mutating paths. ADR-002 documents the design.

In jj-colocated repos, NEVER use 'git push' to push changes. Use 'jj bookmark set <name> -r <change>' then 'jj git push --bookmark <name>'. git push won't see jj changes because the git branch ref isn't updated by jj automatically.

integration-test-shared-container: Memgraph integration tests use a shared container via TestMain in memgraph_test.go. clearDatabase(t) wipes data between tests. Never go back to per-test containers — the difference is 8s vs 280s.

Plan vs registry distinction (Task 32, PR #925): when a plan says 'discover empirically' about a profile mapping or similar registry, ALSO check whether the registry has a stated contract (e.g., a TestClientIDContract that enumerates 'every shipped client'). The plan was right about runtime defect angle (no runtime breakage from omitting specgraph-cli mapping because resources aren't profile-gated, only tools are at internal/mcp/server.go:88-97). But the plan missed the registry-contract angle: the test's docstring at internal/mcp/client_id_contract_test.go:12-15 says it documents every shipped clientInfo.name, so omitting any first-party client violates the contract regardless of current runtime gating. Lesson: 'discover empirically' is necessary but not sufficient when there's an explicit contract test enumerating the registry — runtime gating is one constraint, registry contracts are a separate one.

release-pipeline-lessons: (1) git-cliff-action v4 output is 'version' not 'tag'. (2) actions/checkout persist-credentials:false required when using a different token for push. (3) GitHub rulesets 'Restrict creations' blocks tag pushes even with branch-only targeting — set app to Exempt. (4) Release immutability permanently blocks reuse of deleted tag names. (5) goreleaser v2 uses dockers_v2 not dockers. (6) GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN env vars override gh CLI stored auth — unset them if stale.
Docker is always available for integration tests — locally and in CI. Never suggest deferring integration tests due to Docker unavailability.

feedback-always-brainstorm: Sean wants to brainstorm before implementing, even for seemingly straightforward tasks like repo moves. Don't skip the brainstorming skill — always use it to explore the problem space first.

gh auth gotcha (SpecGraph): seanb4t is the personal account with push access to specgraph/specgraph; the corporate SSO account is the corporate SSO account that doesn't. Both can be authed simultaneously; before pushing or opening a PR run: gh auth switch -u seanb4t -h github.com. Verify with gh auth status — 'Active account: true' should be on the seanb4t row.

vite-plugin-svelte-v4-onmount-bug: @sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte v4.x has a bug where resolve.conditions is set to ['svelte'] only, replacing Vite's default 'browser' condition. This causes 'import { onMount } from svelte' to resolve to index-server.js where onMount=noop, and Rollup tree-shakes the entire callback. Fix: upgrade to v5+ which uses ensureConfigEnvironmentConditions(). onMount works correctly with v5.

dolt-remote-configured: Beads dolt remote pushes to specgraph/specgraph on refs/dolt/data. Use bd dolt push/pull to sync. If pull fails with 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes', run bd dolt commit first.

CodeRabbit reviews against the failing-CI commit, not the latest commit (PR #925 case study). When CI fails on a push, then you fix CI by changing direction (e.g., bumping a CI pin instead of reverting source), CodeRabbit's inline comments will all be tied to the failing commit and tell you to fix things that are now correct on the new state. Always: (1) check what commit hash CodeRabbit reviewed (in its 'Reviewing files that changed... between X and Y' note), (2) cross-reference with PR's current head, (3) empirically verify suggestions before accepting. PR #925 had 5 CodeRabbit inline comments, all stale because CI was bumped from v2.11.1 to v2.12.1 between reviews — verified by: removing the auth_handler.go directives CodeRabbit said were unused → task lint failed with G124 (proving CodeRabbit's 'unused' analysis was for the old version, not current).
jj-cli-flags: Always use --no-pager with jj commands to avoid blocking. Always use -m with jj squash/describe/etc to avoid opening an editor.

Pre-PR validation fragilities encountered running task pr-prep on macOS (Phase B Slice 5 Task 32): (1) lint:go was missing web:build in deps — task clean wipes web/build but web/embed.go declares //go:embed all:build, so golangci-lint typecheck failed. Fixed in commit qlqrryyz. (2) task clean's rm -rf web/node_modules flakes intermittently against pnpm's deeply nested symlink directories with 'Directory not empty' even though nothing else holds them. Manual rm -rf often succeeds on retry. (3) golangci-lint version drift: a v2.12.1 build (vs older local versions that were green during slice development) added gosec G124 (cookie attrs) and tightened nolintlint, surfacing 9 pre-existing failures in unrelated files mid-slice. Worth pinning the lint tool version in task tools. (4) jj diff's default color-words format renders as smushed text without color (e.g., 'specgraph mcp read-resource' deletion + 'specgraph read-mcp-resource' insertion read as 'specgraph read-mcp read-resource' to a Bash tool with stripped ANSI). Use jj diff --git for unambiguous +/- markers when verifying via plain text.

always-fetch-before-branching: ALWAYS run jj git fetch && jj new main before starting any new branch/bookmark. Stale main causes merge conflicts on every PR. This has bitten us repeatedly in the release infra work.

Always run 'task pr-prep' (not just 'task check') before pushing or creating PRs. pr-prep runs check + test:integration + test:e2e and catches tagged test failures that 'task check' misses. Docker is always available.

Key changes: CreateSpec, UpdateSpec, TransitionStage, Store*Output, AmendSpec, LifecycleAmend/Supersede/Abandon, UpdateDecision all use txCtx. ErrConcurrentModification mapped to CodeAborted in decision handler. Version guard added to authoring-level AmendSpec. tx.go has usage guide comment.

content-hash-drift-pr43-merged: PR #43 merged. DEPENDS_ON edges carry content_hash_at_link for drift detection. Timestamp comparison fully replaced with hash comparison.
